hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 9, 8. Your opponent called your raise before the cards were dealt.
Flop — Queen, 9, Jack. You bet. Your opponent called.
River — 6. Your opponent bet large — about the size of the pot.

wrongReasons: Q Q makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
10 5 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
4 J doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only two pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 10 2

Your opponent was drawing to a flush and called the flop to keep their options open. Once they hit, they bet the turn and river for value — growing aggression on later streets is a hallmark of a completed draw.
